Kurals.Couplet: With buds of chilly dew wan evening's shade enclose;My anguish buds space and all my sorrow grows
Kurals.EmotionDetail: , , possibly akin to despair or hopelessness

Kurals.Translation: Lover away, comes eventide Like slayer to field of homicide
Kurals.Explanation: In the absence of my lover, evening comes in like slayers on the field of slaughter
Kurals.Couplet: When absent is my love, the evening hour descends,As when an alien host to field of battle wends

Kurals.Translation: What good have I done to morning And what evil to this evening?
Kurals.Explanation: O eve, why art thou foe! thou dost renew my grief
Kurals.Couplet: O morn, how have I won thy grace? thou bring'st reliefO eve, why art thou foe! thou dost renew my grief

Kurals.Translation: Evening pangs I have not known When my lord nev'r left me alone
Kurals.Explanation: Previous to my husband's departure, I know not the painful nature of evening
Kurals.Couplet: The pangs that evening brings I never knew,Till he, my wedded spouse, from me withdrew

Kurals.Translation: Budding at dawn burgeoning all day This disease blooms in evening gay
Kurals.Explanation: This malady buds forth in the morning, expands all day long and blossoms in the evening
Kurals.Couplet: My grief at morn a bud, all day an opening flower,Full-blown expands in evening hour
Kurals.emotions: Grief – The chapter title and the explanation focus on sorrow related to loss or longing for a beloved

Kurals.Translation: A deadly arm, this shepherd's flute Hails flaming eve and slays my heart
Kurals.Explanation: The shepherd's flute now sounds as a fiery forerunner of night, and is become a weapon that slays (me)
Kurals.Couplet: The shepherd's pipe is like a murderous weapon, to my ear,For it proclaims the hour of ev'ning's fiery anguish near
Kurals.emotions: Grief – The speaker experiences deep sorrow and mourning

Kurals.Translation: Deluding eve if it prolongs The whole town will suffer love-pangs
Kurals.Explanation: When night comes on confusing (everyone's) mind, the (whole) town will lose its sense and be plunged in sorrow
Kurals.Couplet: If evening's shades, that darken all my soul, extend;From this afflicted town will would of grief ascend

Kurals.Translation: Thinking of him whose quest is wealth My life outlives the twilight stealth
Kurals.Explanation: My (hitherto) unextinguished life is now lost in this bewildering night at the thought of him who has the nature of wealth
Kurals.Couplet: This darkening eve, my darkling soul must perish utterly;Remembering him who seeks for wealth, but seeks not me
Kurals.EmotionDetail: Sorrow, Yearning, Confusion

Summary: Highlights enduring moral values and higher truths as guiding virtues.
Encourages cultivating emotions such as Longing, Sorrow, Grief.
Emphasizes moral and spiritual discipline through the ten couplets.
Inspires reflection on righteous living and the pursuit of higher values.

Kurals.Translation: To lift from want he left me afar His thought makes my eyes blush the flower
Kurals.Explanation: While we endure the unbearable sorrow, your eyes weep for him who is gone afar, and shun (the sight of) fragrant flowers
Kurals.Couplet: Thine eyes grown dim are now ashamed the fragrant flow'rs to see,Thinking on him, who wand'ring far, leaves us in misery

Kurals.Translation: My pale tearful eyes betray The hardness of my husband, away
Kurals.Explanation: The discoloured eyes that shed tears profusely seem to betray the unkindness of our beloved
Kurals.Couplet: The eye, with sorrow wan, all wet with dew of tears,As witness of the lover's lack of love appears

Kurals.Translation: These arms that swelled on nuptial day Now shrunk proclaim \"He is away\"
Kurals.Explanation: The shoulders that swelled on the day of our union (now) seem to announce our separation clearly (to the public)
Kurals.Couplet: These withered arms, desertion's pangs abundantly display,That swelled with joy on that glad nuptial day

Kurals.Translation: Bracelets slip off the arms that have Lost old beauty for He took leave
Kurals.Explanation: In the absence of your consort, your shoulders having lost their former beauty and fulness, your bracelets of pure gold have become loose
Kurals.Couplet: When lover went, then faded all their wonted charms,And armlets' golden round slips off from these poor wasted arms
Kurals.emotions: **Loss** – The central focus of the chapter and text is on something precious

Kurals.Translation: Bereft of bracelets and old beauty Arms tell the cruel's cruelty
Kurals.Explanation: The (loosened) bracelets, and the shoulders from which the old beauty has faded, relate the cruelty of the pitiless one
Kurals.Couplet: These wasted arms, the bracelet with their wonted beauty gone,The cruelty declare of that most cruel one
Kurals.emotions: **Sadness** – Reflecting on loss and faded beauty brings a sense of sorrow., **Longing** – There’s a yearning for the past beauty and affection that has been

Kurals.Translation: Arms thin, armlets loose make you call My sire cruel; that pains my soul
Kurals.Explanation: I am greatly pained to hear you call him a cruel man, just because your shoulders are reduced and your bracelets loosened
Kurals.Couplet: I grieve, 'tis pain to me to hear him cruel chid,Because the armlet from my wasted arm has slid
Kurals.emotions: Pain/Sadness – The speaker expresses feeling "greatly pained" by the words of another., Empathy – The speaker is affected on a deep level by the suffering and

Kurals.Translation: Go and tell the cruel, O mind Bruit ov'r my arms and glory find
Kurals.Explanation: Can you O my soul! gain glory by relating to the (so-called) cruel one the clamour of my fading shoulders?
Kurals.Couplet: My heart! say ought of glory wilt thou gain,If to that cruel one thou of thy wasted arms complain
Kurals.emotions: Longing – There is a yearning to communicate feelings to someone perceived as "cruel.", Sadness – The mention of "loss" and "fading shoulders" suggests sorrow over

Kurals.Translation: The front of this fair one O paled As my clasping arms loosed their hold
Kurals.Explanation: When I once loosened the arms that were in embrace, the forehead of the gold-braceleted women turned sallow
Kurals.Couplet: One day the fervent pressure of embracing arms I checked,Grew wan the forehead of the maid with golden armlet decked
Kurals.emotions: Sadness – due to the sense of loss and the sallow

Kurals.Translation: Cool breeze crept between our embrace Her large rain-cloud-eyes paled at once
Kurals.Explanation: When but a breath of breeze penetrated our embrace, her large cool eyes became sallow
Kurals.Couplet: As we embraced a breath of wind found entrance there;The maid's large liquid eyes were dimmed with care
Kurals.emotions: Sadness – The transformation of her eyes from lively to sallow and the intrusion of the breeze suggests a sense of loss or sorrow., Longing – The embrace and the subtle shift

Kurals.Translation: Pale eyes pained seeing the pallor Of the bright forehead of this fair
Kurals.Explanation: Was it at the sight of what the bright forehead had done that the sallowness of her eyes became sad?
Kurals.Couplet: The dimness of her eye felt sorrow now,Beholding what was done by that bright brow

Summary: Highlights enduring moral values and higher truths as guiding virtues.
Encourages cultivating emotions such as Longing, Sadness, Pain.
Emphasizes moral and spiritual discipline through the ten couplets.
Inspires reflection on righteous living and the pursuit of higher values.

Kurals.Translation: Think of, O heart, some remedy To cure this chronic malady
Kurals.Explanation: O my soul, will you not think and tell me some medicine be it what it may, that can cure this incurable malady?
Kurals.Couplet: My heart, canst thou not thinking of some med'cine tell,Not any one, to drive away this grief incurable

Kurals.Translation: Bless O mind! you pine in vain For me he has no love serene
Kurals.Explanation: Is folly, fare thee well my heart!
Kurals.Couplet: Since he loves not, thy smartIs folly, fare thee well my heart

Kurals.Translation: O mind, why pine and sit moody? Who made you so pale lacks pity
Kurals.Explanation: O my soul! why remain (here) and suffer thinking (of him)? There are no lewd thoughts (of you) in him who has caused you this disease of sorrow
Kurals.Couplet: What comes of sitting here in pining thought, O heart? He knowsNo pitying thought, the cause of all these wasting woes

Kurals.Translation: Take these eyes and meet him, O heart Or their hunger will eat me out
Kurals.Explanation: O my soul! take my eyes also with you, (if not), these would eat me up (in their desire) to see him
Kurals.Couplet: O rid me of these eyes, my heart; for they,Longing to see him, wear my life away
Kurals.emotions: Longing/Yearning – The desire to see the beloved is intense and consuming., Love – The emotional connection and affection toward the beloved are central., Anxiety/

Kurals.Translation: He spurns our love and yet, O mind, Can we desert him as unkind?
Kurals.Explanation: O my soul! can he who loves not though he is beloved, be forsaken saying he hates me (now)?
Kurals.Couplet: O heart, as a foe, can I abandon utterlyHim who, though I long for him, longs not for me
Kurals.EmotionDetail: , or Hurt,

Kurals.Translation: Wrath is false, O heart, face-to face Sans huff, you rush to his sweet embrace
Kurals.Explanation: O my soul! when you see the dear one who remove dislike by intercourse, you are displeased and continue to be so Nay, your displeasure is (simply) false
Kurals.Couplet: My heart, false is the fire that burns; thou canst not wrath maintain,If thou thy love behold, embracing, soothing all thy pain

Kurals.Translation: Off with love O mind, or shame I cannot endure both of them
Kurals.Explanation: O my good soul, give up either lust or honour, as for me I can endure neither
Kurals.Couplet: Or bid thy love, or bid thy shame depart;For me, I cannot bear them both, my worthy heart
Kurals.EmotionDetail: , , or longing

Kurals.Translation: Without pity he would depart! You sigh and seek his favour, poor heart!
Kurals.Explanation: You are a fool, O my soul! to go after my departed one, while you mourn that he is not kind enough to favour you
Kurals.Couplet: Thou art befooled, my heart, thou followest him who flees from thee;And still thou yearning criest: 'He will nor pity show nor love to me.'

Kurals.Translation: The lover lives in Self you know; Whom you think, mind to whom you go?
Kurals.Explanation: O my soul! to whom would you repair, while the dear one is within yourself?
Kurals.Couplet: My heart! my lover lives within my mind;Roaming, whom dost thou think to find

Kurals.Translation: Without a thought he deserted us To think of him will make us worse
Kurals.Explanation: If I retain in my heart him who has left me without befriending me, I shall lose even the (inward) beauty that remains
Kurals.Couplet: If I should keep in mind the man who utterly renounces me,My soul must suffer further loss of dignity

Summary: Highlights enduring moral values and higher truths as guiding virtues.
Encourages cultivating emotions such as Longing, Sorrow, Love.
Emphasizes moral and spiritual discipline through the ten couplets.
Inspires reflection on righteous living and the pursuit of higher values.

Kurals.Translation: Passion's axe shall break the door Of reserve bolted with my honour
Kurals.Explanation: The axe of lust can break the door of chastity which is bolted with the bolt of modesty
Kurals.Couplet: Of womanly reserve love's axe breaks through the door,Barred by the bolt of shame before
Kurals.emotions: Desire / Lust, Shame / Modesty, Conflict / Struggle

Kurals.Translation: The thing called lust is a heartless power It sways my mind at midnight hour
Kurals.Explanation: Even at midnight is my mind worried by lust, and this one thing, alas! is without mercy
Kurals.Couplet: What men call love is the one thing of merciless power;It gives my soul no rest, e'en in the midnight hour
Kurals.EmotionDetail: Desire, Worry/Anxiety, Powerlessness

Kurals.Translation: How to hide this lust which shows Itself while I sneeze unawares!
Kurals.Explanation: I would conceal my lust, but alas, it yields not to my will but breaks out like a sneeze
Kurals.Couplet: I would my love conceal, but like a sneezeIt shows itself, and gives no warning sign

Kurals.Translation: I was proud of my sex-reserve Lo lust betrays what I preserve
Kurals.Explanation: I say I would be firm, but alas, my malady breaks out from its concealment and appears in public
Kurals.Couplet: In womanly reserve I deemed myself beyond assail;But love will come abroad, and casts away the veil

Kurals.Translation: Dignity seeks not a deserter But Love-sick is its innovator
Kurals.Explanation: The dignity that would not go after an absent lover is not known to those who are sticken by love
Kurals.Couplet: The dignity that seeks not him who acts as foe,Is the one thing that loving heart can never know

Kurals.Translation: O Grief, my deserter you seek Of your caprice what shall I speak!
Kurals.Explanation: The sorrow I have endured by desiring to go after my absent lover, in what way is it excellent?
Kurals.Couplet: My grief how full of grace, I pray you see!It seeks to follow him that hateth me

Kurals.Translation: When lover's love does what it desires We forget all shame unawares
Kurals.Explanation: I know nothing like shame when my beloved does from love (just) what is desired (by me)
Kurals.Couplet: No sense of shame my gladdened mind shall prove,When he returns my longing heart to bless with love
Kurals.emotions: Top 3 human emotions in the text:, Desire, Love, Vulnerability

Kurals.Translation: The cheater of many wily arts His tempting words break through women's hearts
Kurals.Explanation: Are not the enticing words of my trick-abounding roguish lover the weapon that breaks away my feminine firmness?
Kurals.Couplet: The words of that deceiver, versed in every wily art,Are instruments that break through every guard of woman's heart

Kurals.Translation: In huff I went and felt at ease Heat to heart in sweet embrace
Kurals.Explanation: I said I would feign dislike and so went (away); (but) I embraced him the moment I say my mind began to unite with him!
Kurals.Couplet: 'I 'll shun his greeting'; saying thus with pride away I went:I held him in my arms, for straight I felt my heart relent
Kurals.emotions: Longing – The speaker expresses a sense of yearning and desire for reconnection., Love – The embrace and the unity of minds highlight deep affection., Relief – The speaker feels

Kurals.Translation: To feign dislike is it not rare For mates who melt like fat in fire?
Kurals.Explanation: Is it possible for those whose hearts melt like fat in the fire to say they can feign a strong dislike and remain so?
Kurals.Couplet: 'We 'll stand aloof and then embrace': is this for them to say,Whose hearts are as the fat that in the blaze dissolves away
Kurals.emotions: Longing – Suggests a deep yearning for closeness or connection

Summary: Highlights enduring moral values and higher truths as guiding virtues.
Encourages cultivating emotions such as Longing, Vulnerability, Desire.
Emphasizes moral and spiritual discipline through the ten couplets.
Inspires reflection on righteous living and the pursuit of higher values.

Kurals.Translation: My eyes are dim lustre-bereft Worn fingers count days since he left
Kurals.Explanation: My finger has worn away by marking (on the wall) the days he has been absent while my eyes have lost their lustre and begin to fail
Kurals.Couplet: My eyes have lost their brightness, sight is dimmed; my fingers worn,With nothing on the wall the days since I was left forlorn

Kurals.Translation: Beauty pales and my bracelets slide; Why not forget him now, bright maid?
Kurals.Explanation: O you bright-jewelled maid, if I forget (him) today, my shoulders will lose their beauty even in the other life and make my bracelets loose
Kurals.Couplet: O thou with gleaming jewels decked, could I forget for this one day,Henceforth these bracelets from my arms will slip, my beauty worn away
Kurals.EmotionDetail: Pining/Yearning, ,

Kurals.Translation: Will as guide he went to win Yet I live-to see him again
Kurals.Explanation: I still live by longing for the arrival of him who has gone out of love for victory and with valour as his guide
Kurals.Couplet: On victory intent, His mind sole company he went;And I yet life sustain And long to see his face again

Kurals.Translation: My heart in rapture heaves to see His retun with love to embrace me
Kurals.Explanation: My heart is rid of its sorrow and swells with rapture to think of my absent lover returning with his love
Kurals.Couplet: 'He comes again, who left my side, and I shall taste love's joy,'-My heart with rapture swells, when thoughts like these my mind employ
Kurals.emotions: The top 3 human emotions associated with this text are:, Anticipation, Joy/Rapture, Love

Kurals.Translation: Let me but gaze and gaze my spouse sallow on my soft shoulders files
Kurals.Explanation: May I look on my lover till I am satisfied and thereafter will vanish the sallowness of my slender shoulders
Kurals.Couplet: O let me see my spouse again and sate these longing eyes!That instant from my wasted frame all pallor flies

Kurals.Translation: Let my spouse return just a day Joy-drink shall drive my pain away
Kurals.Explanation: May my husband return some day; and then will I enjoy (him) so as to destroy all this agonizing sorrow
Kurals.Couplet: O let my spouse but come again to me one day!I'll drink that nectar: wasting grief shall flee away
Kurals.EmotionDetail: Pining, , Pain

Kurals.Translation: If my eye-like lord returneth Shall I sulk or clasp or do both?
Kurals.Explanation: On the return of him who is as dear as my eyes, am I displeased or am I to embrace (him); or am I to do both?
Kurals.Couplet: Shall I draw back, or yield myself, or shall both mingled be,When he returns, my spouse, dear as these eyes to me
Kurals.EmotionDetail: , , mixed feelings/ambivalence

Kurals.Translation: May the king fight and win and give And with my wife I will feast this eve!
Kurals.Explanation: Let the king fight and gain (victories); (but) let me be united to my wife and feast the evening
Kurals.Couplet: O would my king would fight, o'ercome, devide the spoil;At home, to-night, the banquet spread should crown the toil
Kurals.emotions: Longing – The speaker expresses a deep desire to be reunited with their beloved., Hope – There is hope for the king’s success and for personal reunion., Joy

Kurals.Translation: One day seems as seven to those Who yearn return of distant spouse
Kurals.Explanation: To those who suffer waiting for the day of return of their distant lovers one day is as long as seven days
Kurals.Couplet: One day will seem like seven to those who watch and yearnFor that glad day when wanderers from afar return

Kurals.Translation: When her heart is broken, what is The good of meeting and love-embrace?
Kurals.Explanation: After (my wife) has died of a broken heart, what good will there be if she is to receive me, has received me, or has even embraced me?
Kurals.Couplet: What's my return, the meeting hour, the wished-for greeting worth,If she heart-broken lie, with all her life poured forth

Summary: Highlights enduring moral values and higher truths as guiding virtues.
Encourages cultivating emotions such as Longing, Hope, Love.
Emphasizes moral and spiritual discipline through the ten couplets.
Inspires reflection on righteous living and the pursuit of higher values.

Kurals.Translation: You hide; but your painted eyes Restraint off, report your surmise
Kurals.Explanation: Though you would conceal (your feelings), your painted eyes would not, for, transgressing (their bounds), they tell (me) something
Kurals.Couplet: Thou hid'st it, yet thine eye, disdaining all restraint,Something, I know not, what, would utter of complaint
Kurals.emotions: Secrecy (or Concealment) – The desire to hide true feelings or intentions., Curiosity (or Suspicion) – The implied act of surmising

Kurals.Translation: With seemly grace and stem-like arms The simple she has ample charms
Kurals.Explanation: Unusually great is the female simplicity of your maid whose beauty fills my eyes and whose shoulders resemble the bamboo
Kurals.Couplet: The simple one whose beauty fills mine eye, whose shoulders curveLike bambu stem, hath all a woman's modest sweet reserve
Kurals.emotions: Admiration – The speaker expresses deep appreciation for the maid’s beauty and simplicity., Awe – The beauty and grace of the maid evoke a sense of wonder., Affection –

Kurals.Translation: Something shines through her jewelled charm Like thread shining through wreathed gem
Kurals.Explanation: There is something that is implied in the beauty of this woman, like the thread that is visible in a garland of gems
Kurals.Couplet: As through the crystal beads is seen the thread on which they 're strungSo in her beauty gleams some thought cannot find a tongue
Kurals.EmotionDetail: for the woman’s beauty and charm, curiosity about the implied or hidden message,

Kurals.Translation: Like scent in bud secrets conceal In the bosom of her half smile
Kurals.Explanation: There is something in the unmatured smile of this maid like the fragrance that is contained in an unblossomed bud
Kurals.Couplet: As fragrance in the opening bud, some secret liesConcealed in budding smile of this dear damsel's eyes

Kurals.Translation: The close-bangled belle's hidden thought Has a cure for my troubled heart She to Her Maid
Kurals.Explanation: The well-meant departure of her whose bangles are tight-fitting contains a remedy that can cure my great sorrow
Kurals.Couplet: The secret wiles of her with thronging armlets decked,Are medicines by which my raising grief is checked
Kurals.emotions: Top 3 associated emotions:, Longing – The speaker expresses desire for relief from their sorrow

Kurals.Translation: His over-kind close embrace sooths; But makes me feel, loveless, he parts
Kurals.Explanation: The embrace that fills me with comfort and gladness is capable of enduring (my former) sorrow and meditating on his want of love
Kurals.Couplet: While lovingly embracing me, his heart is only grieved:It makes me think that I again shall live of love bereaved

Kurals.Translation: Quick, my bracelets read before The mind of my lord of cool shore
Kurals.Explanation: My bracelets have understood before me the (mental) separation of him who rules the cool seashore
Kurals.Couplet: My severance from the lord of this cool shore,My very armlets told me long before
Kurals.emotions: Longing – The speaker senses an emotional or mental distance from a loved one., Sadness – There is a sense of sorrow or melancholy over the separation., Intuition –

Kurals.Translation: My lover parted but yesterday; With sallowness it is seventh day The Maid Tells Him
Kurals.Explanation: It was but yesterday my lover departed (from me); and it is seven days since my complexion turned sallow
Kurals.Couplet: My loved one left me, was it yesterday?Days seven my pallid body wastes away

Kurals.Translation: She views her armlets, her tender arms And then her feet; these are her norms
Kurals.Explanation: She looked at her bracelets, her tender shoulders, and her feet; this was what she did there (significantly)
Kurals.Couplet: She viewed her tender arms, she viewed the armlets from them slid;She viewed her feet: all this the lady did
Kurals.emotions: Anticipation – The act of looking at her own ornaments and body parts may suggest she is waiting or preparing for something

Kurals.Translation: To express love-pangs by eyes and pray Is womanhood's womanly way
Kurals.Explanation: To express their love-sickness by their eyes and resort to begging bespeaks more than ordinary female excellence
Kurals.Couplet: To show by eye the pain of love, and for relief to pray,Is womanhood's most womanly device, men say

Summary: Highlights enduring moral values and higher truths as guiding virtues.
Encourages cultivating emotions such as Longing, Admiration, Sadness.
Emphasizes moral and spiritual discipline through the ten couplets.
Inspires reflection on righteous living and the pursuit of higher values.

Kurals.Translation: Rapture at thought and joy when seen Belong to love and not to wine
Kurals.Explanation: To please by thought and cheer by sight is peculiar, not to liquor but lust
Kurals.Couplet: Gladness at the thought, rejoicing at the sight,Not palm-tree wine, but love, yields such delight
Kurals.emotions: Joy – Expressed as "rapture at thought" and "cheer by sight.", Desire – Referenced as love or lust

Kurals.Translation: When passion grows palmyra-tall Sulking is wrong though millet-small
Kurals.Explanation: If women have a lust that exceeds even the measure of the palmyra fruit, they will not desire (to feign) dislike even as much as the millet
Kurals.Couplet: When as palmyra tall, fulness of perfect love we gain,Distrust can find no place small as the millet grain
Kurals.EmotionDetail: Strong longing or passion, Fondness or attachment in a relationship, Reflecting on the idea that sulking is unnecessary or inappropriate

Kurals.Translation: Though slighting me he acts his will My restless eyes would see him still
Kurals.Explanation: Though my eyes disregard me and do what is pleasing to my husband, still will they not be satisfied unless they see him
Kurals.Couplet: Although his will his only law, he lightly value me,My heart knows no repose unless my lord I see

Kurals.Translation: Huff I would, maid, but I forget; And leap to embrace him direct
Kurals.Explanation: My heart, forgetting all, could not its love restrain
Kurals.Couplet: My friend, I went prepared to show a cool disdain;My heart, forgetting all, could not its love restrain

Kurals.Translation: When close I see not lord's blemish Like eyes that see not painter's brush
Kurals.Explanation: Like the eyes which see not the pencil that paints it, I cannot see my husband's fault (just) when I meet him
Kurals.Couplet: The eye sees not the rod that paints it; nor can ISee any fault, when I behold my husband nigh
Kurals.emotions: Love – A deep attachment and affection

Kurals.Translation: When he's with me I see not fault And nought but fault when he is not
Kurals.Explanation: When I see my husband, I do not see any faults; but when I do not see him, I do not see anything but faults
Kurals.Couplet: When him I see, to all his faults I 'm blind;But when I see him not, nothing but faults I find
Kurals.emotions: The top 3 human emotions associated with this text are:, Love – The speaker's affection for her husband is clear in her appreciation of him when they are together., Longing – There is a sense of yearning or missing her husband

Kurals.Translation: To leap in stream which carries off When lord is close to feign a huff
Kurals.Explanation: Like those who leap into a stream which they know will carry them off, why should a wife feign dislike which she knows cannot hold out long?
Kurals.Couplet: As those of rescue sure, who plunge into the stream,So did I anger feign, though it must falsehood seem
Kurals.emotions: Distress – Reflects the emotional pain or discomfort experienced in the situation., Longing – Indicates a deep desire or yearning for union or reconciliation., Helplessness – Suggest

Kurals.Translation: Like wine to addicts that does disgrace Your breast, O thief, is for my embrace!
Kurals.Explanation: O you rogue! your breast is to me what liquor is to those who rejoice in it, though it only gives them an unpleasant disgrace
Kurals.Couplet: Though shameful ill it works, dear is the palm-tree wineTo drunkards; traitor, so to me that breast of thine
Kurals.emotions: Longing/Desire – The speaker expresses a strong yearning for the embrace., Shame/Disgrace – There is a comparison to wine that brings disgrace

Kurals.Translation: Flower-soft is love; a few alone Know its delicacy so fine
Kurals.Explanation: Sexual delight is more delicate than a flower, and few are those who understand its real nature
Kurals.Couplet: Love is tender as an opening flower In season dueTo gain its perfect bliss is rapture known to few
Kurals.emotions: Delicacy – Reflecting sensitivity and gentleness in love., Longing – Implied through the appreciation of an experience known by few., Intimacy – The emphasis

Kurals.Translation: She feigned dislike awhile but flew Faster for embrace than I do
Kurals.Explanation: She once feigned dislike in her eyes, but the warmth of her embrace exceeded my own
Kurals.Couplet: Her eye, as I drew nigh one day, with anger shone:By love o'erpowered, her tenderness surpassed my own

Kurals.Translation: You see, his heart is his alone; Why not my heart be all my own?
Kurals.Explanation: O my soul! although you have seen how his soul stands by him, how is it you do not stand by me?
Kurals.Couplet: You see his heart is his aloneO heart, why not be all my own
Kurals.emotions: Longing – There is a deep yearning for emotional connection and reciprocity., Jealousy – The speaker envies the beloved’s self-attachment and questions why their own heart cannot be

Kurals.Translation: O heart, you see how he slights me Yet you clasp him as if friendly
Kurals.Explanation: O my soul! although you have known him who does not love me, still do you go to him, saying "he will not be displeased."
Kurals.Couplet: 'Tis plain, my heart, that he 's estranged from thee;Why go to him as though he were not enemy

Kurals.Translation: You follow him at will Is it \"The fallen have no friends\" my heart?
Kurals.Explanation: O my soul! do you follow him at pleasure under the belief that the ruined have no friends?
Kurals.Couplet: 'The ruined have no friends, 'they say; and so, my heart,To follow him, at thy desire, from me thou dost depart
Kurals.emotions: Longing – There is a sense of yearning for connection and understanding from the beloved., Sadness – The feeling of being "ruined" or "fallen" and lacking friends conveys

Kurals.Translation: You won't sulk first and then submit Who will then consult you, my heart?
Kurals.Explanation: O my soul! you would not first seem sulky and then enjoy (him); who then would in future consult you about such things?
Kurals.Couplet: 'See, thou first show offended pride, and then submit,' I bade;Henceforth such council who will share with thee my heart

Kurals.Translation: Frets to gain and fears loss in gain O my heart suffers ceaseless pain
Kurals.Explanation: My soul fears when it is without him; it also fears when it is with him; it is subject to incessant sorrow
Kurals.Couplet: I fear I shall not gain, I fear to lose him when I gain;And thus my heart endures unceasing pain
Kurals.EmotionDetail: fear of loss and fear even in togetherness, ceaseless pain and incessant sorrow,

Kurals.Translation: My itching mind eats me anon As I muse on him all alone
Kurals.Explanation: My mind has been (here) in order to eat me up (as it were) whenever I think of him in my solitude
Kurals.Couplet: My heart consumes me when I ponder lone,And all my lover's cruelty bemoan
Kurals.emotions: Longing – The speaker intensely desires and misses "the beloved

Kurals.Translation: I forget shame but not his thought In mean foolish mind I'm caught
Kurals.Explanation: I have even forgotten my modesty, having been caught in my foolish mind which is not dignified enough to forget him
Kurals.Couplet: Fall'n 'neath the sway of this ignoble foolish heart,Which will not him forget, I have forgotten shame

Kurals.Translation: My heart living in love of him Hails his glory ignoring blame
Kurals.Explanation: My soul which clings to life thinks only of his (own) gain in the belief that it would be disgraceful for it to despise him
Kurals.Couplet: If I contemn him, then disgrace awaits me evermore;My soul that seeks to live his virtues numbers o'er

Kurals.Translation: Who support a man in grief If lover's heart denies relief?
Kurals.Explanation: Who would help me out of one's distress, when one's own soul refuses help to one?
Kurals.Couplet: And who will aid me in my hour of grief,If my own heart comes not to my relief

Kurals.Translation: Why wonder if strangers disown When one's own heart is not his own?
Kurals.Explanation: It is hardly possible for strangers to behave like relations, when one's own soul acts like a stranger
Kurals.Couplet: A trifle is unfriendliness by aliens shown,When our own heart itself is not our own

Kurals.Translation: Feign sulk; embrace him not so that We can see his distress a bit
Kurals.Explanation: Let us witness awhile his keen suffering; just feign dislike and embrace him not
Kurals.Couplet: Be still reserved, decline his profferred love;A little while his sore distress we 'll prove
Kurals.emotions: Hurt/Sadness – The text describes someone's keen suffering and distress., Longing/Desire – There is an implied yearning for affection or connection., Playfulness – The

Kurals.Translation: Sulking is the salt of love; but Too much of it spoils the taste Wife Addresses Husband
Kurals.Explanation: A little dislike is like salt in proportion; to prolong it a little is like salt a little too much
Kurals.Couplet: A cool reserve is like the salt that seasons well the mess,Too long maintained, 'tis like the salt's excess
Kurals.emotions: Affection/Love – The text frames sulking as related to the dynamics of love., Displeasure/Irritation – Sulking and "a little dislike" suggest mild negative

Kurals.Translation: To leave the sulker unembraced Is to grieve the one sorely grieved
Kurals.Explanation: For men not to embrace those who have feigned dislike is like torturing those already in agony
Kurals.Couplet: 'Tis heaping griefs on those whose hearts are grieved;To leave the grieving one without a fond embrace

Kurals.Translation: To comfort not lady in pout Is to cut the fading plant at root He within Himself
Kurals.Explanation: Not to reconcile those who have feigned dislike is like cutting a faded creeper at its root
Kurals.Couplet: To use no kind conciliating art when lover grieves,Is cutting out the root of tender winding plant that droops
Kurals.emotions: Sadness – The imagery of fading and cutting at the root evokes a deep sense of sorrow and loss., Regret – The notion of failing to comfort implies missed opportunities and a sense

Kurals.Translation: Pouting of flower-eyed has To pure good mates a lovely grace
Kurals.Explanation: An increased shyness in those whose eyes are like flowers is beautiful even to good and virtuous husbands
Kurals.Couplet: Even to men of good and worthy mind, the petulanceOf wives with flowery eyes lacks not a lovely grace

Kurals.Translation: Love devoid of frowns and pets Misses its ripe and unripe fruits
Kurals.Explanation: Sexual pleasure, without prolonged and short-lived dislike, is like too ripe, and unripe fruit
Kurals.Couplet: Love without hatred is ripened fruit;Without some lesser strife, fruit immature
Kurals.emotions: Top 3 emotions associated with the text:, Longing, Displeasure, Love

Kurals.Translation: \"Will union take place soon or late?\" In lover's pout this leaves a doubt
Kurals.Explanation: The doubt as to whether intercourse would take place soon or not, creates a sorrow (even) in feigned dislike
Kurals.Couplet: A lovers' quarrel brings its pain, when mind afraidAsks doubtful, 'Will reunion sweet be long delayed?'

Kurals.Translation: What's the good of grieving lament When concious lover is not present?
Kurals.Explanation: What avails sorrow when I am without a wife who can understand the cause of my sorrow?
Kurals.Couplet: What good can grieving do, when none who loveAre there to know the grief thy soul endures

Kurals.Translation: Water delights in a shady grove And sulking in souls of psychic love
Kurals.Explanation: Like water in the shade, dislike is delicious only in those who love
Kurals.Couplet: Water is pleasant in the cooling shade;So coolness for a time with those we love
Kurals.EmotionDetail: or Sulking, ,

Kurals.Translation: My heart athirst would still unite With her who me in sulking left!
Kurals.Explanation: It is nothing but strong desire that makes her mind unite with me who can leave her to her own dislike
Kurals.Couplet: Of her who leaves me thus in variance languishing,To think within my heart with love is fond desire
Kurals.emotions: Longing (or Desire) – The heart is athirst and yearns for reunion., Abandonment (or Hurt) – There is pain in being left or sulked by

Kurals.Translation: I shrink to clasp you bosom lewd To the gaze of all ladies exposed
Kurals.Explanation: You are given to prostitution; all those who are born as womankind enjoy you with their eyes in an ordinary way I will not embrace you
Kurals.Couplet: From thy regard all womankind Enjoys an equal grace;O thou of wandering fickle mind, I shrink from thine embrace
Kurals.emotions: Shame – There is a sense of public exposure and embarrassment., Rejection – The speaker refuses to embrace the subject

Kurals.Translation: He sneezed while we went on sulking Expecting me to say \"live long\"
Kurals.Explanation: When I continued to be sulky he sneezed and thought I would (then) wish him a long life
Kurals.Couplet: One day we silent sulked; he sneezed: The reason well I knew;He thought that I, to speak well pleased, Would say, 'Long life to you!'
Kurals.EmotionDetail: resentment, ,

Kurals.Translation: \"For which lady?\" she widely cries While I adorn myself with flowers
Kurals.Explanation: Even if I were adorned with a garland of branch-flowers, she would say I did so to show it to another woman
Kurals.Couplet: I wreathed with flowers one day my brow, The angry tempest lowers;She cries, 'Pray, for what woman now Do you put on your flowers?'

Kurals.Translation: \"I love you more than all\" I said \"Than whom, than whom?\" she sulked and chid
Kurals.Explanation: When I said I loved her more than any other woman, she said "more than others, yes, more than others," and remained sulky
Kurals.Couplet: 'I love you more than all beside,' 'T was thus I gently spoke;'What all, what all?' she instant cried; And all her anger woke
Kurals.emotions: Insecurity – The person doubts their place in the speaker's affections

Kurals.Translation: \"In this life we won't part\" I told Her eyes at once with tears were filled
Kurals.Explanation: When I said I would never part from her in this life her eyes were filled with tears
Kurals.Couplet: 'While here I live, I leave you not,' I said to calm her fearsShe cried, 'There, then, I read your thought'; And straight dissolved in tears

Kurals.Translation: I said I thought of you She left Her embrace crying \"Oft you forget\"
Kurals.Explanation: When I said I had remembered her, she said I had forgotten her and relaxing her embrace, began to feign dislike
Kurals.Couplet: 'Each day I called to mind your charms,' 'O, then, you had forgot,'She cried, and then her opened arms, Forthwith embraced me not

Kurals.Translation: I sneezed; she blessed; then changed and wept \"You sneezed now at which lady's thought?\"
Kurals.Explanation: When I sneezed she blessed me, but at once changed (her mind) and wept, asking, "At the thought of whom did you sneeze?"
Kurals.Couplet: She hailed me when I sneezed one day; But straight with anger seized,She cried; 'Who was the woman, pray, Thinking of whom you sneezed?'
Kurals.emotions: Jealousy – The woman's immediate suspicion about whom the sneeze was "for" suggests jealousy., Insecurity – Her quick change from blessing to weeping reflects underlying insecurity or fear

Kurals.Translation: I repressed sneeze; she wept crying \"Your thoughts from me you are hiding\"
Kurals.Explanation: When I suppressed my sneezing, she wept saying, "I suppose you (did so) to hide from me your own people's remembrance of you"
Kurals.Couplet: And so next time I checked my sneeze; She forthwith wept and cried,(That woman difficult to please), 'Your thoughts from me you hide'

Kurals.Translation: I try to coax her and she remarks \"Your coaxing others thus this marks\"
Kurals.Explanation: 'I see', quoth she, 'to other folks How you are wondrous kind' Even when I try to remove her dislike, she is displeased and says, "This is the way you behave
Kurals.Couplet: I then began to soothe and coax, To calm her jealous mind;'I see', quoth she, 'to other folks How you are wondrous kind
Kurals.emotions: **Jealousy** – The speaker’s attempts at coaxing are met with suspicion that such kindness is shown to others as well

Kurals.Translation: I think and gaze at her; she chides: \"On whom your thought just now abides?\"
Kurals.Explanation: Even when I look on her contemplating (her beauty), she is displeased and says, "With whose thought have you (thus) looked on my person?"
Kurals.Couplet: I silent sat, but thought the more, And gazed on her Then sheCried out, 'While thus you eye me o'er, Tell me whose form you see'
Kurals.emotions: Top 3 human emotions associated with the text are:, Jealousy, Insecurity, Longing

Kurals.Translation: He is flawless; but I do pout So that his loving ways show out
Kurals.Explanation: Although my husband is free from defects, the way in which he embraces me is such as to make me feign dislike
Kurals.Couplet: Although there be no fault in him, the sweetness of his loveHath power in me a fretful jealousy to move
Kurals.emotions: Love – The affection and admiration for the husband’s loving nature., Playfulness – The act of feigning dislike to elicit a loving response., Longing/Desire

Kurals.Translation: Fading first, love blooms and outlives The petty pricks that pouting gives
Kurals.Explanation: His love will increase though it may (at first seem to) fade through the short-lived distress caused by (my) dislike
Kurals.Couplet: My 'anger feigned' gives but a little pain;And when affection droops, it makes it bloom again
Kurals.EmotionDetail: , distress, forgiveness/renewal

Kurals.Translation: Is there a heaven like sulk beneath Of hearts that join like water and earth?
Kurals.Explanation: Is there a celestial land that can please like the feigned dislike of those whose union resembles that of earth and water?
Kurals.Couplet: Is there a bliss in any world more utterly divine,Than 'coyness' gives, when hearts as earth and water join
Kurals.emotions: Longing – for unity or heavenly reconciliation., Contentment – derived from the harmony after discord., Affection – the underlying love or bond despite apparent sulking or feigned

Kurals.Translation: In long pout after embrace sweet A weapon is up to break my heart
Kurals.Explanation: In prolonged dislike after an embrace there is a weapon that can break my heart
Kurals.Couplet: 'Within the anger feigned' that close love's tie doth bind,A weapon lurks, which quite breaks down my mind
Kurals.emotions: Hurt – There is a sense of pain and vulnerability

Kurals.Translation: Though free form faults, one feels the charms Of feigned release from lover's arms
Kurals.Explanation: Though free from defects, men feel pleased when they cannot embrace the delicate shoulders of those whom they love
Kurals.Couplet: Though free from fault, from loved one's tender armsTo be estranged a while hath its own special charms
Kurals.EmotionDetail: , or Delight,

Kurals.Translation: Sweeter than meal is digestion And sulk in love than union
Kurals.Explanation: To digest what has been eaten is more delightful than to eat more; likewise love is more delightful in dislike than intercourse
Kurals.Couplet: 'Tis sweeter to digest your food than 'tis to eat;In love, than union's self is anger feigned more sweet
Kurals.emotions: Longing, Joy, Reconciliation Explanation: The text expresses the joy found in the resolution

Kurals.Translation: The yielder wins in lover's pout Reunited joy brings it out
Kurals.Explanation: Those are conquerors whose dislike has been defeated and that is proved by the love (which
Kurals.Couplet: In lovers' quarrels, 'tis the one that first gives way,That in re-union's joy is seen to win the day

Kurals.Translation: Shall not our pouting again give The dew-browed joy of joint love?
Kurals.Explanation: Will I enjoy once more through her dislike, the pleasure of that love that makes her forehead perspire?
Kurals.Couplet: And shall we ever more the sweetness know of that embraceWith dewy brow; to which 'feigned anger' lent its piquant grace
Kurals.emotions: Longing – The speaker expresses a desire to experience the joy of love once again., Hope – There is an anticipation of reconciliation and renewed intimacy., Regret – The mention

Kurals.Translation: Sulk on O belle of shining jewels! Prolong O night! our delight swells!
Kurals.Explanation: May the bright-jewelled one feign dislike, and may the night be prolonged for me to implore her!
Kurals.Couplet: Let her, whose jewels brightly shine, aversion feignThat I may still plead on, O night, prolong thy reign
Kurals.emotions: Longing – The speaker wishes for the night to be prolonged

Kurals.Translation: Bouderie is lovers' delight Its delight grows when they unite
Kurals.Explanation: Dislike adds delight to love; and a hearty embrace (thereafter) will add delight to dislike
Kurals.Couplet: A 'feigned aversion' coy to pleasure gives a zest;The pleasure's crowned when breast is clasped to breast
Kurals.emotions: Top 3 human emotions associated with the text are:, Love, Delight, Reconciliation
Kurals.EmotionDetail: , , , or Forgiveness
